content:
# {{projectName}} - Oracle Database Project

This is an Oracle database project created using the CLI tool.

## Project Structure

```
{{projectName}}/
├── data/           # Persistent data storage
├── migrations/     # Database migrations
├── seeds/         # Seed data
├── config/        # Configuration files
├── scripts/       # Utility scripts
├── docker-compose.yml
├── .env
└── README.md
```

## Getting Started

1. Make sure you have Docker and Docker Compose installed on your system.
2. Review and modify the `.env` file with your desired settings.
3. Start the database:
   ```bash
   docker compose up -d
   ```
4. The Oracle database will be available at `localhost:1521`

## Default Credentials

- SYS User: sys
- SYS Password: (set in .env)
- System Identifier (SID): {{projectName}}
- Pluggable Database (PDB): {{projectName}}_pdb

## Connecting to the Database

You can connect to the database using SQL*Plus or any Oracle client with the following connection details:

- Host: localhost
- Port: 1521
- Service Name: ORCLCDB
- SYS Password: (set in .env)

Example connection using SQL*Plus:
```bash
sqlplus sys/oracle@localhost:1521/ORCLCDB as sysdba
```

## Database Migrations

Place your SQL migration files in the `migrations/` directory. Files will be executed in alphabetical order when the container is first created.

## Seed Data

Place your seed data SQL files in the `seeds/` directory. These will be executed after migrations.

## Data Persistence

All database data is stored in the `data/` directory and will persist between container restarts.

## Stopping the Database

To stop the database:

```bash
docker compose down
```

## Troubleshooting

1. Check container logs:
   ```bash
   docker compose logs oracle
   ```
2. Access container shell:
   ```bash
   docker compose exec oracle bash
   ```
3. Connect to SQL*Plus:
   ```bash
   docker compose exec oracle sqlplus sys/oracle as sysdba
   ```

## Important Notes

- The Oracle database container may take several minutes to start up for the first time.
- The default character set is set to AL32UTF8 for Unicode support.
- The container uses Oracle Database 19c Enterprise Edition.
- Make sure you have sufficient disk space as Oracle databases can be quite large.
